pattern: Greedy monotone stack: keep the result lexicographically smallest by popping larger characters when a smaller one arrives and the popped character still appears later.
hints: Count how many times each character appears in the remaining suffix; this tells you whether it is safe to discard a character now.

Maintain a stack and a 'used' set. For each character, if it is already in the result skip it. Otherwise pop from the stack any character that is larger than the current one AND still has future occurrences.
Decrement each character's remaining count as you move through the string. Push the current character onto the stack only after finishing the pop loop, then mark it as used.

edge_inputs.description: all characters already in sorted order — no pops needed
all same character — result is single char
classic case where a later smaller char forces popping earlier larger chars
last occurrence must be kept even though larger
reverse sorted string — each later char is smaller but last occurrence forces retention
long run of one letter before others

stress.description: random lowercase string to stress greedy stack logic
